description_long: This is a vintage, single-engine monoplane with a high-mounted straight wing and a fixed taildragger landing gear. The fuselage is slender and cylindrical, with a forward-placed, enclosed cockpit featuring a clear canopy. The wings are painted a light gray and display the registration code 'NX-211' in bold black letters. The nose houses a radial piston engine with a two-blade propeller, and the tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The overall appearance suggests a classic aircraft design, reminiscent of early 20th-century aviation.
description: A vintage high-wing monoplane with a single radial engine, fixed taildragger gear, and bold registration markings on the wings.
description_view_0: Rear three-quarter view showing the tail section, high-mounted wing, and registration code on the left wing.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the radial engine, propeller, forward cockpit, and landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear right view focusing on the tail, high wing, and the right side of the fuselage.
description_view_3: Direct rear view displaying the tail assembly, landing gear, and both wings with visible registration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing monoplane with straight, unswept wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted radial piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy integrated into the fuselage.
